title: Pack two 4-byte error selectors in one uint256, select by condition using computed offset
trigger_patterns: two possible errors from one check
token does not exist or wrong owner
description: Two 4-byte selectors packed into one uint256 constant. The revert offset is computed as shl(2, condition) — when condition=0 the offset is 0 (first selector at 0x1c), when condition=1 the offset is 4 (second selector at 0x18, landing at 0x1c after the 4-byte shift). Branch-less — no JUMPI needed to pick the error.
pattern_before:
if (owner == address(0)) revert TokenDoesNotExist();
if (owner != from) revert TransferFromIncorrectOwner();
when_to_apply: Two mutually exclusive error conditions that can be detected from the same values, where one condition implies the other is impossible
when_not_to_apply: More than two possible errors, errors requiring parameters, when conditions are not mutually exclusive
